name: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
description: The largest art museum in the western United States, with an encyclopedic collection of over 150,000 works spanning 6,000 years of art from across the globe.

name: The Broad
description: A contemporary art museum founded by Eli and Edythe Broad, housing nearly 2,000 works of postwar and contemporary art. General admission is always free.

name: Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
description: One of the largest natural and cultural history museums in the world, with collections of nearly 35 million specimens and artifacts spanning 4.5 billion years of history.

name: California Science Center
description: A hands-on science museum and one of the largest science centers in the western United States, home to the Space Shuttle Endeavour.

name: Museum of Contemporary Art (MOCA)
description: The only museum in Los Angeles devoted exclusively to contemporary art from the 1940s to the present, with nearly 7,500 works in its permanent collection.

name: The Getty Center
description: Designed by Richard Meier, the Getty Center houses European paintings, drawings, sculpture, illuminated manuscripts, decorative arts, and photographs. Admission is free.
